title: Variables: var, final, const
description: Learn mutable and immutable bindings in Dart with type inference.

concept.definition: Dart supports var for mutable variables, final for single-assignment runtime values, and const for compile-time constants.
concept.analogy: var is a writable note, final is a one-time ink note, const is a printed label.
keyPoints: Use var for values that change
Use final for values assigned once
Type inference works from assigned values

title: Core Data Types
description: Use int, double, String, bool, List, and Map in Dart.

concept.definition: Dart is type-safe and supports both inferred and explicit types for common primitives and collections.
concept.analogy: Types are containers with shape rules for stored values.

title: Functions
description: Define typed functions, optional parameters, and arrow syntax.

concept.definition: Functions in Dart can use explicit return types, positional/named parameters, defaults, and concise arrow bodies.
concept.analogy: A function is a machine with typed inputs and predictable outputs.

title: Control Flow
description: Branch with if/else and switch to direct program execution.

concept.definition: Control flow statements route execution based on conditions and values.
concept.analogy: Conditions are traffic lights for code paths.

title: Loops
description: Iterate with for, while, and for-in over collections.

concept.definition: Dart offers multiple loop styles for counted iteration, conditional iteration, and collection traversal.
concept.analogy: Loops are repeat tracks with controlled exits.
keyPoints: for is ideal for indexed loops
while repeats while condition is true

title: Collections
description: Work with List, Set, and Map plus transform methods.

concept.definition: Dart collections provide typed containers and expressive APIs like map, where, and fold.
concept.analogy: Collections are shelves; methods help filter and reshape items.

title: Null Safety
description: Use nullable types, ?, !, and ?? to handle absence safely.

concept.definition: Dart null safety distinguishes nullable and non-nullable types at compile time.
concept.analogy: Nullable types are marked boxes that may be empty.

title: Classes
description: Model data and behavior with class fields and methods.

concept.definition: Classes encapsulate related state and behavior in reusable object blueprints.
concept.analogy: A class is a template; objects are created instances.
keyPoints: Use types on fields and methods

title: Constructors
description: Use default, named, and factory constructors in Dart.

concept.definition: Constructors create instances and can provide alternate creation paths with named or factory forms.
concept.analogy: Constructors are different entry doors to the same class.
keyPoints: factory constructors can return cached instances
Initializer lists run before constructor body

title: Inheritance & Mixins
description: Reuse behavior with extends, implements, and mixins.

concept.definition: Dart supports single inheritance plus interfaces and mixins for flexible code reuse.
concept.analogy: Inheritance is a family tree; mixins are skill add-ons.

title: Async/Await
description: Handle asynchronous code with Future, async, and await.

concept.definition: Dart async code uses Future values and await for readable asynchronous flows.
concept.analogy: Future is a promise box that gets filled later.

title: Error Handling
description: Use try/catch/finally and throw exceptions safely.

concept.definition: Dart handles failures with exceptions and structured recovery blocks.
concept.analogy: try/catch is a safety harness around risky code.
